gaps: A way to see the corpus's disposition-label mapping for historical merits cases (what 'other' vs 'dismissed' concretely mean for writ-of-error-era matters) without opening the outcome-bearing opinion cluster.
Term/decade metadata on the snapshot or via stats (term_year is unset for nearly all historical SCOTUS priors), which would let base rates be cut by era instead of pooled.
helpful: fedcourts stats --court scotus gave a decisive reference-class base rate for a fact-free snapshot.
Prior predictors' committed predictions/reasoning under data/cases made the house label conventions legible.
notes: Snapshot had no case-specific signal beyond the caption and one cluster; the whole prediction rests on the reference class.
